goal: Review the proof and issues for q9; agree on the key remaining gaps and an action plan.

messages.author: lek-heng-lim
messages.body:
Let me open by reframing what this problem is really asking, because the algebraic geometry here is cleaner than the notation suggests.

We have $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ built from $4\times 4$ determinants of stacked rows of $3\times 4$ matrices. The key observation is that each $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ is itself a *decomposable* object in a precise sense: it lives in $\bigwedge^4(\mathbb{R}^4)$-flavored Plücker world, since you are taking $4\times 4$ minors of a $12\times 4$ configuration. So the "generic" hypothesis on the $A^{(i)}$ is buying us that the $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ are in general position on the relevant Segre/secant variety.

The real content of the third bullet is this: we want to detect, by polynomials of $n$-independent degree, exactly when the scaling tensor $\lambda \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times n\times n\times n}$ is **rank-one off the diagonal**, i.e. $\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}=u_\alpha v_\beta w_\gamma x_\delta$. That is nothing but asking for the defining equations of the *Segre variety* $\mathrm{Seg}(\mathbb{P}^{n-1}\times\cdots\times\mathbb{P}^{n-1})$, restricted to the off-diagonal locus, but pulled back through the linear map $\lambda \mapsto (\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta} Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)})$.

So my first questions for the room:
1. The Segre variety of rank-one tensors is cut out scheme-theoretically by the $2\times 2$ minors — degree-2 equations, $n$-independent in degree. Good. But here we cannot observe $\lambda$ directly; we only observe $\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$. The crux is whether we can *recover* the $2\times 2$ minor relations on $\lambda$ from the observed tensors — i.e. whether the map $\lambda\mapsto \lambda\cdot Q$ is injective enough on the off-diagonal entries.
2. Are the $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ linearly independent as we range over distinct $(\alpha,\beta,\gamma,\delta)$? If they are, then $\mathbf{F}$ can simply read off each scalar $\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}$ via a fixed linear projection (independent of $A$ thanks to genericity being a Zariski-open guarantee), and then apply the $2\times 2$ Segre minors. That would settle existence affirmatively.
3. Over $\mathbb{R}$ versus $\mathbb{C}$: the Segre $2\times2$-minor ideal is the same, but the "$\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}\neq 0$ iff not identical" support condition is a real semialgebraic constraint. Does the iff in bullet three need the *reality* of $u,v,w,x$? If so we must be careful — sign obstructions can appear, exactly as real rank exceeds complex rank.

My instinct: the answer is **yes**, $\mathbf{F}$ exists, and it is essentially the pullback of the $2\times2$ Segre min

messages.author: giorgio-ottaviani
messages.body:
Lek-Heng is right that the target is the $2\times 2$ minor ideal of $\mathrm{Seg}(\mathbb{P}^{n-1})^{\times 4}$ off the diagonal, but I want to insist on his question 2, because that is where the whole construction lives or dies. We must ask: what is the dimension of the linear span of $\{Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}\}$ inside $\mathbb{R}^{3\times3\times3\times3}$, and is the map $\lambda \mapsto (\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta} Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)})$ recoverable by a *fixed* linear projection?

Here is the geometric heart. By construction $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)} = a^{(\alpha)}\boxtimes a^{(\beta)}\boxtimes a^{(\gamma)}\boxtimes a^{(\delta)}$ where each $A^{(\alpha)}$, a $3\times 4$ matrix, defines via its $4\times 4$ minors a Plücker/Veronese-type map $\mathbb{R}^3 \to \bigwedge^4\mathbb{R}^4 \cong \mathbb{R}$ after contracting against the fixed remaining rows. More precisely $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}_{ijk\ell}$ is the $\bigwedge^4$ of four rows drawn from four $3\times 4$ blocks — so $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ is a **rank-one** element of $(\mathbb{R}^3)^{\otimes 4}$ *only* in the sense that it factors through the wedge, and the genericity of the $A^{(i)}$ guarantees that the $81$-dimensional ambient space $\mathbb{R}^{3\times3\times3\times3}$ receives these in linearly general position. Note $\binom{12}{4}=495$ governs the available Plücker coordinates, comfortably more than $81$, so generically there is no forced collinearity.

But linear independence is *not* automatic and not even necessary in the form Lek-Heng wants — and this is exactly an Apolarity question. Frame it this way: define $X = \mathrm{Seg}(\mathbb{P}^{n-1}\times\mathbb{P}^{n-1}\times\mathbb{P}^{n-1}\times\mathbb{P}^{n-1})$ and let $\phi:\mathbb{R}^{n^4}\to \mathbb{R}^{81}$ be the linear map $\lambda \mapsto \sum \lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ — wait, Lek-Heng is right that we do *not* sum, we observe each scaled slice. So the genuine object is the **diagonal-supported** restriction. The real question is whether the assignment $(\alpha,\beta,\gamma,\delta)\mapsto Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ admits, for each off-diagonal index, a fixed dual functional $D_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}\in (\mathbb{R}^{81})^*$ *independent of $A$* with $D_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}(Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)})=\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}$. That is imp

messages.author: lek-heng-lim
messages.body:
Giorgio gets to the crux but I want to correct the framing, because there is a subtlety we are both glossing over: the dual functional $D_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}$ *cannot* be independent of $A$ in the naive sense, and that is precisely why this is a real problem and not a triviality.

Let me be careful. We observe the $81$-dimensional slice $\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ for each off-diagonal multi-index. To extract $\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}$ we do *not* need an $A$-independent functional — we need $\mathbf{F}$ itself to be $A$-independent. These are different requirements. Here is the move that saves us:

1. **Scale-invariant ratios.** Each $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ is a fixed nonzero vector in $\mathbb{R}^{81}$ (generically nonzero by the genericity hypothesis — this is a Zariski-open condition, exactly the kind I always insist on checking). So $\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ determines $\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}$ *up to* the fixed unknown vector $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$. We cannot read off $\lambda$ absolutely without knowing $A$. But the Segre equations are *homogeneous* — the $2\times2$ minors $\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}\lambda_{\alpha'\beta'\gamma'\delta'} - \lambda_{\alpha\beta'\gamma\delta}\lambda_{\alpha'\beta\gamma'\delta'}$ are degree-2 and multilinear in distinct slices.

2. **The trick is to take a degree-2 contraction across two slices.** Consider the bilinear pairing $\langle \lambda_{\mu}Q^{(\mu)}, \lambda_{\nu}Q^{(\nu)}\rangle = \lambda_\mu \lambda_\nu \langle Q^{(\mu)}, Q^{(\nu)}\rangle$ for a fixed bilinear form (say the Frobenius inner product on $\mathbb{R}^{81}$, which is $A$-independent). Then a $2\times2$ Segre minor in $\lambda$ becomes a *ratio of such contractions*, and the unknown Gram factors $\langle Q^{(\mu)},Q^{(\nu)}\rangle$ are what obstruct $A$-independence. So Giorgio's apolarity question is exactly: **do the Gram entries $\langle Q^{(\mu)},Q^{(\nu)}\rangle$ factor in a way compatible with the Plücker structure so that they cancel in the minor relations?**

3. This is where the $4\times4$-minor (Plücker) origin matters. Because $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}_{ijk\ell}$ is multilinear and *separately* indexed by $\alpha,\beta,\gamma,\delta$, the Gram tensor $\langle Q^{(\mu)},Q^{(\nu)}\rangle$ does **not** generically factor as a product — and that is the genuine obstruction. The question is whether one can find higher-degree but still **$n$-independent-degree** polynomials that cancel these

messages.author: giorgio-ottaviani
messages.body:
Let me sharpen the Gram-factorization question, because I think Lek-Heng has identified the real obstruction but stated it slightly too pessimistically.

The key structural fact is that $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ is **genuinely multilinear in the four indices**: it factors through a single linear map $L:\mathbb{R}^3\otimes\mathbb{R}^4 \to \mathbb{R}^3$ applied blockwise. Concretely, write $a^{(\alpha)}_i = A^{(\alpha)}(i,:)\in\mathbb{R}^4$. Then
$$Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}_{ijk\ell} = \det(a^{(\alpha)}_i, a^{(\beta)}_j, a^{(\gamma)}_k, a^{(\delta)}_\ell) = \langle a^{(\alpha)}_i \wedge a^{(\beta)}_j \wedge a^{(\gamma)}_k \wedge a^{(\delta)}_\ell,\ \omega\rangle$$
where $\omega\in\bigwedge^4\mathbb{R}^4\cong\mathbb{R}$ is the volume form. So $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)} = (B^{(\alpha)}\otimes B^{(\beta)}\otimes B^{(\gamma)}\otimes B^{(\delta)})$ contracted with $\omega$, where $B^{(\alpha)}:\mathbb{R}^3\hookrightarrow\mathbb{R}^4$ is the embedding given by the rows of $A^{(\alpha)}$. This is **precisely a Tucker decomposition with factor matrices $A^{(\alpha)}$ and core the alternating tensor $\omega$** — which is exactly what the problem statement asserts with $\mathbf{C}$ being the $\det$-core.

Now here is the geometric point that resolves Lek-Heng's worry. The Gram entries $\langle Q^{(\mu)}, Q^{(\nu)}\rangle$ do *not* factor — correct — but **we should not be working with the Frobenius pairing at all.** The right move is Apolarity. Consider the multilinear functional: because $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$ is alternating-multilinear, the family $\{Q^{(\mu)}\}$ spans a space carrying a $GL_3^{\times 4}$-equivariant structure, and the off-diagonal $2\times 2$ Segre minors on $\lambda$ correspond to specific $GL$-isotypic components. The question "does $\mathbf{F}$ exist of $n$-independent degree" is then: **is the off-diagonal Segre ideal generated in bounded degree after pullback through $\phi$?** By the determinantal/Plücker rigidity, the pullback of the degree-2 Segre minors lands in a *fixed* $GL_3^{\times 4}$-module independent of $n$ — the index $n$ only multiplies the number of copies, not the degree.

So my concrete proposal, and a question for the room:

1. The recovery obstruction is not absolute recovery of $\lambda_{\mu}$ but recovery *up to a fixed nonzero scalar per slice*, $c_\mu = \|Q^{(\mu)}\|$ in whatever fixed pairing we pick. A degree-2 Segre minor $\lambda_\mu\lambda_{\mu'} - \lambda_\

messages.author: coordinator
messages.body:
**Action plan synthesized** (8 steps):

Prove existence of a bounded-degree polynomial map F that detects off-diagonal rank-one structure of the scaling tensor lambda by pulling back the 2x2 Segre minor ideal through the linear map lambda -> (lambda_{αβγδ} Q^{(αβγδ)}), exploiting the Plücker/Tucker structure of the Q tensors.

plan.summary: Prove existence of a bounded-degree polynomial map F that detects off-diagonal rank-one structure of the scaling tensor lambda by pulling back the 2x2 Segre minor ideal through the linear map lambda -> (lambda_{αβγδ} Q^{(αβγδ)}), exploiting the Plücker/Tucker structure of the Q tensors.

steps.title: Formalize the Q-tensor as a Tucker/Plücker object
steps.body: Rigorously establish the identity Q^{(αβγδ)}_{ijkl} = det(a^{(α)}_i, a^{(β)}_j, a^{(γ)}_k, a^{(δ)}_l) = contraction of B^{(α)}⊗B^{(β)}⊗B^{(γ)}⊗B^{(δ)} with the volume form ω, confirming it is a Tucker decomposition with factor matrices A^{(i)} and alternating det-core. Verify the genuine separate multilinearity in the four indices α,β,γ,δ.

steps.title: Verify genericity is a Zariski-open guarantee
steps.body: Show that under the generic hypothesis on the A^{(i)}, each Q^{(αβγδ)} is a nonzero vector in R^{81} (using binom(12,4)=495 > 81 to rule out forced collinearity), and characterize precisely the Zariski-open condition required. Determine the dimension of the linear span of {Q^{(αβγδ)}} inside R^{3x3x3x3}.

steps.title: Resolve the recovery-up-to-scalar obstruction
steps.body: Address the central crux: we observe lambda_{μ}Q^{(μ)}, recovering lambda_μ only up to the fixed unknown scalar c_μ = ||Q^{(μ)}|| in a fixed A-independent pairing. Prove that the homogeneous degree-2 Segre 2x2 minors lambda_μ lambda_{μ'} - lambda_{μ''} lambda_{μ'''} can be expressed via bilinear contractions of observed slices, and identify exactly which Gram factors <Q^{(μ)},Q^{(ν)}> must cancel.

steps.title: Analyze Gram-factorization via GL-equivariant structure
steps.body: Investigate whether the off-diagonal 2x2 Segre minors, pulled back through phi: lambda -> sum lambda_{μ}Q^{(μ)}, land in a fixed GL_3^{×4}-isotypic module independent of n. Use Plücker/determinantal rigidity to argue the index n only multiplies the number of module copies, not the polynomial degree. Settle whether the Gram entries factor compatibly or require higher (but n-independent) degree polynomials to cancel.

steps.title: Construct the explicit polynomial map F of bounded degree
steps.body: Assemble the map F from the pulled-back Segre minors that detects when lambda is rank-one off the diagonal (lambda_{αβγδ} = u_α v_β w_γ x_δ). Confirm the degree of F is independent of n, and that F is A-independent as required. Provide the explicit construction including how the unknown scalars c_μ are eliminated.

steps.title: Handle the real vs complex distinction
steps.body: Examine the third bullet's iff condition over R versus C. Determine whether the support condition (lambda_{αβγδ} ≠ 0 iff not identical) requires reality of u,v,w,x, and whether sign obstructions analogous to real-rank-exceeds-complex-rank appear. Adapt F to the real semialgebraic constraint if necessary.

steps.title: Stress-test the construction for hidden gaps
steps.body: Critically review the full argument: check that the bilinear contractions are genuinely A-independent, that the Segre ideal is generated in bounded degree after pullback, and that no edge cases (degenerate slices, diagonal indices, vanishing Gram entries) break the construction. Flag any step where n-independence of degree silently fails.

steps.title: Final verification and assembly of the proof
steps.body: Verify the complete chain of reasoning yields a correct affirmative answer that F exists with n-independent degree. Confirm each lemma (Tucker identity, genericity openness, scalar elimination, GL-equivariant degree bound, real-case handling) is sound and assemble into a coherent final proof.
